title: hypericum salsugineum
intro: Hypericum salsugineum, also known as salt-leaved St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant species within the St. John's Wort family (Hypericaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, as well as providing relief from wounds, burns, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. Hypericum salsugineum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
